title: Moving Cities — Trading Stability for Optionality
problem: Deciding to move to a new city involves dozens of sub-decisions — neighbourhood, timing, what to keep, what to leave behind. Each one has a rationale that feels obvious at the time and opaque six months later. Without a record, the move just becomes "I moved" with no trace of the thinking behind it.

lineage.spawnReason: Relocating cities was the second major life decision tied to the career pivot — both were made together and deserve linked provenance records.

analysis: Moving cities is less like a single decision and more like a project: there's a top-level choice (go or stay) and then a cascade of sub-decisions that follow from it. The LAC parallel is exact — a root feature spawns children as the scope becomes clear. The relocation itself was the root: the neighbourhood, the lease length, the timing were the children. None were documented. The pattern is that life decisions have natural lineage too — they just aren't usually thought of that way.

decisions.decision: Optimise for optionality over stability at this stage
decisions.rationale: Early-career, no dependents, low switching cost. The asymmetry favoured moving: upside was access to a denser network and more opportunities; downside was temporary discomfort.
alternativesConsidered: Stay in current city — lower risk but compounding opportunity cost
Move abroad — too high a switching cost, language barrier for professional networking

decisions.decision: Short-term lease for the first 6 months
decisions.rationale: Committing to a long lease before understanding the city's geography would have been premature optimisation. Short-term preserved the option to relocate within the city once the real constraints were visible.
alternativesConsidered: 12-month lease immediately — cheaper per month but locked in without local knowledge
Co-living / furnished rooms — too transient, hard to focus

decisions.rationale: January moves happen at the same time as everyone else's — inflated prices, depleted inventory. Mid-year had better optionality in the rental market.
alternativesConsidered: January — cleaner psychological reset but worse market conditions
Wait until year-end — too long, decision fatigue was already high

implementation: No code. The feature.json models the decision structure: one root choice (move) with sub-decisions (when, where, how long) that mirror the way software features spawn children as scope clarifies.
knownLimitations: Sub-decisions (neighbourhood, lease terms) aren't spawned as separate child features here — that would be the full application of the model
Emotional and relational factors aren't easily captured in the decisions schema — the model handles logic better than feeling
successCriteria: A reader sees that the structure of a relocation decision is identical to the structure of a software architectural decision — and that documenting both is worth doing for the same reasons.
